metadata.description: 经典 CNN 架构: LeNet/AlexNet/VGG/ResNet/Inception. These are all well-known deep learning architectures in the field of computer vision. Here's a brief explanation for each: - **LeNet**: One of the earliest convolutional neural networks (CNNs) designed specifically for handwritten digit recognition. ```python from keras.models import Sequential from keras.layers import Conv2D, MaxPooling2D, Flatten, Dense model = Sequential() model.add(Conv2D(6, kernel_size=5, activation='tanh', input_shape=(32, 32, 1))) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=2)) model.add(Conv2D(16, kernel_size=5, activation='tanh')) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=2)) model.add(Flatten()) model.add(Dense(120, activation='tanh')) model.add(Dense(84, activation='tanh')) model.add(Dense(10, activation='softmax')) # For recognizing digits from 0 to 9 ``` - **AlexNet**: The first deep learning model that achieved significant success in the ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge (ILSVRC) in 2012. ```python from keras.models import Sequential from keras.layers import Conv2D, MaxPooling2D, Flatten, Dense model = Sequential() model.add(Conv2D(96, kernel_size=11, strides=4, activation='relu', input_shape=(227, 227, 3))) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=2)) model.add(Conv2D(256, kernel_size=5, padding='same', activation='relu')) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=2)) model.add(Conv2D(384, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu')) model.add(Conv2D(384, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu')) model.add(Conv2D(256, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu')) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=2)) model.add(Flatten()) model.add(Dense(4096, activation='relu')) model.add(Dense(4096, activation='relu')) model.add(Dense(1000, activation='softmax')) # For recognizing ImageNet classes ``` - **VGG**: A family of convolutional neural networks that achieved state-of-the-art performance on the ILSVRC in 2014. ```python from keras.models import Sequential from keras.layers import Conv2D, MaxPooling2D, Flatten, Dense model = Sequential() for i in range(10): if (i+1) % 2 == 0: model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=(2, 2), strides=2)) else: model.add(Conv2D(64, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu')) model.add(Flatten()) model.add(Dense(4096, activation='relu')) model.add(Dense(4096, activation='relu')) model.add(Dense(1000, activation='softmax')) # For recognizing ImageNet classes ``` - **ResNet**: A deep residual network that introduced the concept of "residual blocks" to address the vanishing gradient problem in very deep networks. ```python from keras.models import Model from keras.layers import Input, Conv2D, BatchNormalization, Activation, Add def residual_block(input_tensor): x = Conv2D(64, kernel_size=3, padding='same')(input_tensor) x = BatchNormalization()(x) x = Activation('relu')(x) x = Conv2D(64, kernel_size=3, padding='same')(x) x = Add()([input_tensor, x]) return x input_layer = Input(shape=(224, 224, 3)) x = Conv2D(64, kernel_size=7, strides=2, activation='relu')(input_layer) x = MaxPooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=2)(x) for _ in range(9): x = residual_block(x) model = Model(inputs=input_layer, outputs=x) # For building a ResNet-18 ``` - **Inception**: A network architecture that uses multiple parallel paths with different filter sizes and pooling layers to increase the receptive field of the network. ```python from keras.models import Sequential from keras.layers import Conv2D, MaxPooling2D, AveragePooling2D, Dense model = Sequential() model.add(Conv2D(64, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu', input_shape=(299, 299, 3))) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=2)) model.add(Conv2D(192, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu')) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=2)) # Inception module def inception_module(input_tensor): branch_0 = Conv2D(64, kernel_size=1, activation='relu')(input_tensor) branch_1 = Conv2D(96, kernel_size=1, activation='relu')(input_tensor) branch_1 = Conv2D(128, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu')(branch_1) branch_2 = Conv2D(16, kernel_size=1, activation='relu')(input_tensor) branch_2 = Conv2D(32, kernel_size=5, padding='same', activation='relu')(branch_2) branch_pool = AveragePooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=1)(input_tensor) branch_pool = Conv2D(32, kernel_size=1, activation='relu')(branch_pool) output_tensor = Concatenate()([branch_0, branch_1, branch_2, branch_pool]) return output_tensor x = inception_module(model.output) model.add(Flatten()) model.add(Dense(1000, activation='softmax')) # For recognizing ImageNet classes ``` These examples provide a basic structure for each network. Note that the actual implementation details and hyperparameters may vary based on specific requirements and use cases.

data_pointers.title: 经典 CNN 架构
data_pointers.content_preview: 经典 CNN 架构: LeNet/AlexNet/VGG/ResNet/Inception. These are all well-known deep learning architectures in the field of computer vision. Here's a brief explanation for each: - **LeNet**: One of the earliest convolutional neural networks (CNNs) designed specifically for handwritten digit recognition. ```python from keras.models import Sequential from keras.layers import Conv2D, MaxPooling2D, Flatten, Dense model = Sequential() model.add(Conv2D(6, kernel_size=5, activation='tanh', input_shape=(32, 32, 1))) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=2)) model.add(Conv2D(16, kernel_size=5, activation='tanh')) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=2)) model.add(Flatten()) model.add(Dense(120, activation='tanh')) model.add(Dense(84, activation='tanh')) model.add(Dense(10, activation='softmax')) # For recognizing digits from 0 to 9 ``` - **AlexNet**: The first deep learning model that achieved significant success in the ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge (ILSVRC) in 2012. ```python from keras.models import Sequential from keras.layers import Conv2D, MaxPooling2D, Flatten, Dense model = Sequential() model.add(Conv2D(96, kernel_size=11, strides=4, activation='relu', input_shape=(227, 227, 3))) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=2)) model.add(Conv2D(256, kernel_size=5, padding='same', activation='relu')) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=2)) model.add(Conv2D(384, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu')) model.add(Conv2D(384, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu')) model.add(Conv2D(256, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu')) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=2)) model.add(Flatten()) model.add(Dense(4096, activation='relu')) model.add(Dense(4096, activation='relu')) model.add(Dense(1000, activation='softmax')) # For recognizing ImageNet classes ``` - **VGG**: A family of convolutional neural networks that achieved state-of-the-art performance on the ILSVRC in 2014. ```python from keras.models import Sequential from keras.layers import Conv2D, MaxPooling2D, Flatten, Dense model = Sequential() for i in range(10): if (i+1) % 2 == 0: model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=(2, 2), strides=2)) else: model.add(Conv2D(64, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu')) model.add(Flatten()) model.add(Dense(4096, activation='relu')) model.add(Dense(4096, activation='relu')) model.add(Dense(1000, activation='softmax')) # For recognizing ImageNet classes ``` - **ResNet**: A deep residual network that introduced the concept of "residual blocks" to address the vanishing gradient problem in very deep networks. ```python from keras.models import Model from keras.layers import Input, Conv2D, BatchNormalization, Activation, Add def residual_block(input_tensor): x = Conv2D(64, kernel_size=3, padding='same')(input_tensor) x = BatchNormalization()(x) x = Activation('relu')(x) x = Conv2D(64, kernel_size=3, padding='same')(x) x = Add()([input_tensor, x]) return x input_layer = Input(shape=(224, 224, 3)) x = Conv2D(64, kernel_size=7, strides=2, activation='relu')(input_layer) x = MaxPooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=2)(x) for _ in range(9): x = residual_block(x) model = Model(inputs=input_layer, outputs=x) # For building a ResNet-18 ``` - **Inception**: A network architecture that uses multiple parallel paths with different filter sizes and pooling layers to increase the receptive field of the network. ```python from keras.models import Sequential from keras.layers import Conv2D, MaxPooling2D, AveragePooling2D, Dense model = Sequential() model.add(Conv2D(64, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu', input_shape=(299, 299, 3))) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=2)) model.add(Conv2D(192, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu')) model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=2)) # Inception module def inception_module(input_tensor): branch_0 = Conv2D(64, kernel_size=1, activation='relu')(input_tensor) branch_1 = Conv2D(96, kernel_size=1, activation='relu')(input_tensor) branch_1 = Conv2D(128, kernel_size=3, padding='same', activation='relu')(branch_1) branch_2 = Conv2D(16, kernel_size=1, activation='relu')(input_tensor) branch_2 = Conv2D(32, kernel_size=5, padding='same', activation='relu')(branch_2) branch_pool = AveragePooling2D(pool_size=3, strides=1)(input_tensor) branch_pool = Conv2D(32, kernel_size=1, activation='relu')(branch_pool) output_tensor = Concatenate()([branch_0, branch_1, branch_2, branch_pool]) return output_tensor x = inception_module(model.output) model.add(Flatten()) model.add(Dense(1000, activation='softmax')) # For recognizing ImageNet classes ``` These examples provide a basic structure for each network. Note that the actual implementation details and hyperparameters may vary based on specific requirements and use cases.
